title: scirpus lacustris subsp. duvalii
intro: Scirpus lacustris subsp. duvalii, also known as Duval's bulrush, is a subspecies of the common reed that is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in some cosmetic products due to its antioxidant properties.
